data.body:
G4 container child-consistency audit (parent f89d-e54d-203d-4ee0 × THIS child 822a "Honest bidirectional round-trip probe; de-encode 'expected-bug' guards"), all 7 cross-child interaction modes checked against the WHOLE sibling roster (2359/33d0/59ef/8937/fe57) under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter, repo-corroborated. VERDICT: clean pairing — no G4 cross-child finding.

Repo corroboration of 822a's premises and surfaces:
- The probe guard 822a de-encodes exists verbatim: scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh L1543 `fail_test "Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap" "unexpectedly synced inbound"` (SYNCED branch) + L1546 `pass_test "...inbound NOT implemented, as expected"` + L1547 `matrix_set "comments" "inbound" "update" "NOT-SYNCED"` (not-synced branch); L1914 "inbound NOT implemented — known gap, shown as NOT-SYNCED." A repo search for `issuelinks` under scripts/jira-pressure-test returned NO matches — corroborating 822a's premise "no probe round-trips relationships at all." No sibling edits the probe scripts, so 822a's audit target is live.
- The bidirectional behavior 822a's hermetic round-trip asserts is ALREADY present in production (the epic closes a TEST/PROBE-coverage seam, not re-fixes the bugs): inbound `_diff_links_inbound` is wired (inbound_differ.py L440/L712) and the fetcher merges `issuelinks` into each snapshot entry (fetcher.py L450-454, bug-3f04 enrichment); the production batch path update_one routes parent via client.set_parent (batch_dispatch.py L400-403), plus comments and links (L387-388 comment confirms "the production outbound dispatch routes through this legacy batch path, NOT the typed leaf"). So 822a's comment/link/parent round-trip is assertable against the CURRENT production path without an unmet sibling-delivered fix.

7-mode walk for THIS pairing:
- Implicit shared state — BENIGN. 822a file_impact = scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh (de-encode guards) + tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_roundtrip.py (new). DISJOINT from every sibling: the outbound-applier cluster (33d0→apply_outbound.py/batch_dispatch.py/applier.py; 2359→batch_dispatch.py/applier.py/reconcile.py) and the schema/fixture/contract files (8937→_snapshot_schema.py/test_snapshot_contract.py/fetcher.py; fe57→_fakes.py/fixtures/capture script; 59ef→test_acliclient_contract.py/acli_graph.py) are all disjoint. No sibling touches the probe script or test_roundtrip.py. No shared-file co-edit.
- Conflicting assumptions — BENIGN. 822a asserts inbound↔outbound round-trip OUTCOMES (did comment/link/parent sync) through "the production path"; consistent with fe57 (fixtures flow through fetch_snapshot unmassaged), 8937 (snapshot-entry schema), and 33d0 (production batch applier). 822a does NOT round-trip labels and does NOT read the `labels_applied` sub-op payload, so the outbound labels list-vs-count representation conflict tracked on the 33d0↔2359 outbound-telemetry pairing is outbound-internal and untouched by 822a.
- Dependency gap — BENIGN. 822a's only input dependency — story A's fixtures/fake — is correctly declared via depends_on fe57. The producer↔consumer schema/contract (8937) is explicitly scoped OUT and is not required to stand up a behavioral round-trip. The inbound `comment`/`issuelinks` reads and outbound batch link/comment/parent dispatch its round-trip exercises already exist in production (bug-0ee6/3f04 fixes landed), so 822a has no unmet "fix-must-land-first" edge to a sibling. 822a binds to "the production path" ABSTRACTLY, so it requires no hard 33d0-before-822a edge: against current code the production batch path already carries the dispatch its round-trip asserts, and after 33d0's collapse the survivor IS the production path by construction (same abstract-binding pattern ruled benign for keystone 8937, applied symmetrically here).
- Scope overlap — BENIGN. 33d0 AC (outbound-only combined multi-sub-op UPDATE asserting each sub-op took effect = consolidation correctness), 8937 AC3 (assembled snapshot-entry CONTRACT through fetcher→differ→applier, test_snapshot_contract.py), and 822a AC1 (BIDIRECTIONAL inbound↔outbound comment/link/parent ROUND-TRIP, test_roundtrip.py) exercise the same production path from distinct angles on distinct files — complementary, not duplicative. 822a explicitly scopes the schema/contract OUT (→8937).
- Ordering violation — BENIGN. 822a's edited files are disjoint from the 33d0↔2359 same-region outbound-applier sequencing cluster. The only hard ordering edge (fixtures before the test that uses them) is captured by depends_on fe57; no hard "33d0-before-822a" sequence is required for 822a's assertions to be meaningful (abstract production-path binding; the behavior is already in code).
- Consumer impact — BENIGN. 822a AC3 keeps the hermetic round-trip in CI and any live variant opt-in/scheduled/documented (consistent with the epic's "live e2e is overkill / never flakes CI" posture and parent "no net increase in brittle tests"); AC4 asserts round-trip OUTCOMES, not whole-blob equality, imposing no brittle expectation on consumers.
- Residual references — BENIGN. The `Phase3.verify-inbound-comments-gap … NOT-SYNCED` guard 822a converts exists verbatim in the repo (L1543/1546/1547); no sibling deletes the probe scripts under scripts/jira-pressure-test, so its audit target is live. 33d0's deletion of the dead typed-leaf applier strands nothing 822a references — 822a drives the production batch path, not the typed leaf.

The roster-level parent anti-change-detector-guardrail matter is a G3 coverage question (and per the parent AC's explicit "(owned by story B)" clause is owned by 8937) — not a G4 cross-child inconsistency, so not addressed here.

VERDICT for this pairing (parent × 822a): no G4 cross-child finding; all 7 interaction modes read benign under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.
